---
title: "Paisano + Burgundy 2020"
startDate: "2020-07-10T00:00:00.000Z"
endDate: "2020-07-11T00:00:00.000Z"
tripPlan: content/trip-plans/paisano-burgundy.mdx
---

## Gear

All the gear we brought was useful! The doubles I brought seemed useful, the nuts were definitely useful, radios were useful since belays ended on top ridges.

- #0.2 - #4, with doubles of #0.4 - #2
- Nuts
- 9 single alpines, 2 double alpines, 2 sport draws
- Radios (useful)
- 4 liters of water
- Bug spray (tons of mosquitos at camp)

## Friday - To camp

We backpacked in, leaving the car at 5:00 PM, and arriving at 7:15 PM. It went faster than expected, and it was even hot so was walking slow!

![The turn off of the main trail](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200710_173253.osawIZIj-768x1024.jpg)
![The start is right at the center at the tree](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200710_181925.vamgocyC-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_043446.iqWS9pai-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_045909.n7rs6ONU-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_050446.1Eq6aHiA-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_050503.08WCzK74-1024x768.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_051655.brsbgYX-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_054044.fS4nw1gZ-1024x768.jpg)

## Pitch 1

A bit of battling trees, a little leanback crack climbing. One section where I had to grab a low hanging tree branch to get past a slabby section (Alexis did that too). Not the most enjoyable.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_052235.pyyWp2q-768x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0514285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:651,&quot;y&quot;:396},{&quot;x&quot;:554,&quot;y&quot;:341},{&quot;x&quot;:489,&quot;y&quot;:302},{&quot;x&quot;:431,&quot;y&quot;:251},{&quot;x&quot;:433,&quot;y&quot;:223},{&quot;x&quot;:421,&quot;y&quot;:182}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false},{&quot;it&quot;:5,&quot;is&quot;:1.0514285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;x&quot;:497,&quot;y&quot;:213,&quot;t&quot;:&quot;Grab tree&quot;,&quot;ta&quot;:&quot;l&quot;,&quot;tb&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_054035.F2XWqwTx-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 2

The second pitch scrambles some 3rd/4th class terrain to the base of a short 5.7 crack. But some people climbed that crack in that pitch, so I did too. Next time I'd recommend just belaying at the base, and then you can climb the short crack as part of P3.

![Top of 5.7 crack, Alexis at belay](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_063022.TcNcevUd-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_064340.jfZySRBU-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 3

Above the 5.7 crack, the route wanders around the right and then back around the left on easier terrain (although I imagine if you placed pieces, rope drag could become bad). Ends at the iconic 5.8+/5.9- twin crack pitch.

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_064409.fxFAmFnh-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_064412.qarwIAIb-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 4

Finally some climbing. Crux for me was above the twin cracks. I found two nut placements, but then there's some hunting for some slabby moves (I think there was some stemming involved). Probably more of a mental crux.

Once at the top, I traversed and slightly downclimbed to the left to the base of the next pitch (which seemed better than starting the next pitch with the traverse).

![The twin cracks](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_070833.Vnu3Ez7-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_070855.8VSLwmsW-1024x768.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_075135.U7JZ9yX-768x1024.jpg)
![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_075817.A6zBCLa-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 5

This was some enjoyable climbing, climbs up the corner for a bit and then some face climbing. A bit of hunting for holds, and possibly not too much gear, but overall decently fun. Ends on top the ridge.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_080359.rZPFejDj-768x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0514285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:2,&quot;y&quot;:823},{&quot;x&quot;:111,&quot;y&quot;:695},{&quot;x&quot;:222,&quot;y&quot;:593},{&quot;x&quot;:297,&quot;y&quot;:538},{&quot;x&quot;:337,&quot;y&quot;:492},{&quot;x&quot;:390,&quot;y&quot;:427},{&quot;x&quot;:426,&quot;y&quot;:343},{&quot;x&quot;:474,&quot;y&quot;:300},{&quot;x&quot;:556,&quot;y&quot;:211},{&quot;x&quot;:603,&quot;y&quot;:141}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Pitch 6

Just some more scrambling. At one point, you can go left or right around a rock on the ridge... apparently you're supposed to go right (which looked worse), and then traverse 20 feet to the right to find the next pitch.

I went left, which looked pretty travelled. I ended the belay right below where the route steepened again, however I possibly should have climbed the next little steep section, as the next pitch I just climbed up that section and then belayed again.

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_085446.tiTs7SWS-768x1024.jpg"

## Pitch 7

Off route. Short little 30' or so

![](https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_092707.h2xAriCj-768x1024.jpg)

## Pitch 8

Off route. I thought this was the "twin cracks" but apparently not. Either way, the climbing felt within grade and with almost the full rope length made it to the top!

<RouteOverlay 
  imageSrc="https://andrewbares.blob.core.windows.net/hiking-blog-images/2020/07/IMG_20200711_093255.0moh92g9-768x1024.jpg" 
  topoData="{&quot;items&quot;:[{&quot;it&quot;:0,&quot;is&quot;:1.0514285714285714,&quot;ic&quot;:&quot;#ffff00&quot;,&quot;ia&quot;:1,&quot;lw&quot;:3,&quot;cp&quot;:[{&quot;x&quot;:301,&quot;y&quot;:1013},{&quot;x&quot;:322,&quot;y&quot;:700},{&quot;x&quot;:302,&quot;y&quot;:512},{&quot;x&quot;:330,&quot;y&quot;:443},{&quot;x&quot;:380,&quot;y&quot;:340},{&quot;x&quot;:393,&quot;y&quot;:306},{&quot;x&quot;:402,&quot;y&quot;:300},{&quot;x&quot;:415,&quot;y&quot;:266},{&quot;x&quot;:425,&quot;y&quot;:242}],&quot;nl&quot;:10,&quot;fl&quot;:10,&quot;lc&quot;:false}]}"
  topoOverlaySrc="data:image/png;base64,"
/>

## Summit

10:15 AM on the top!
